criteria: The agent has read Section 4 (Method) completely to understand the two core components of MARINE: visual guidance from image-grounded features and guided text generation via classifier-free guidance.

The agent has read Section 4.1 to understand that MARINE uses DETR and RAM++ as additional image-grounded models, aggregates their detected objects via intersection, and prepends the guidance prompt 'focusing on the visible objects in this image:' to the aggregated object list.

The agent has read Section 4.2 and Algorithm 1 to understand the logit-space guided generation formula: log p̂(y_t | v, c, x, y<t) = γ * log p(y_t | v, c, x, y<t) + (1-γ) * log p(y_t | v, x, y<t), where γ is the guidance strength.

The agent has read Section 5.1 to understand the five LVLM models evaluated (LLaVA, LLaVA-v1.5, MiniGPT-v2, mPLUG-Owl2, InstructBLIP) and the hyperparameter settings (γ=0.7, DETR score threshold=0.95, RAM++ detection threshold=0.68, greedy sampling, random seed=242, max token length=64).
The plan includes loading DETR and RAM++ as external vision models for object detection guidance, and combining their outputs using intersection of detected objects.

The plan includes implementing the logit-space guided generation as described in Algorithm 1, performing two forward passes per token (one with and one without the guidance prompt c) and combining their log-probabilities.
The plan includes setting the MARINE hyperparameters: guidance strength γ=0.7, DETR score threshold=0.95, RAM++ detection threshold=0.68, greedy sampling, random seed=242, and max token length=64.
The plan includes evaluating MARINE across all five LVLM architectures (LLaVA, LLaVA-v1.5, MiniGPT-v2, mPLUG-Owl2, InstructBLIP) on CHAIR, POPE, and GPT-4V-aided evaluation metrics.

The plan includes implementing all baselines: Greedy-Decoding, LURE, Woodpecker, VCD (with α=1, adaptive plausibility threshold=0.1, noise step=500), and OPERA (with scale factor θ=50, retrospection threshold=25, beam size=5).
The DETR model is loaded and used for object detection with a score confidence threshold of 0.95 to filter detected objects.
The RAM++ model is loaded and used for object recognition with a detection threshold of 0.68 to filter recognized object tags.

The intersection of detected objects from DETR and RAM++ is computed (using synonym matching via NLTK) and formatted as a guidance prompt by prepending 'focusing on the visible objects in this image:' to the aggregated object list.

The guided generation at each decoding step performs two separate forward passes through the LVLM: one with the guidance prompt c (conditional) and one without c (unconditional), producing two sets of logits.

The final logit at each decoding step is computed as the linear combination: ℓ(t) = γ * ℓ_cond(t) + (1-γ) * ℓ_uncond(t), where γ=0.7, and the next token is sampled from this combined logit space using greedy decoding.

The prompt templates for MARINE-intersection guidance (Table 9) are implemented, with a randomly selected template from the available list used per query to format the detected objects into the guidance context.

The CHAIR metric is implemented to evaluate hallucination: CHAIRI = |hallucinated objects| / |all mentioned objects| and CHAIRS = |captions with hallucinated objects| / |all captions|, using the 80 MSCOCO object categories and the synonym list from Lu et al. (2018).
The Recall metric is implemented as Recall = |non-hallucinated objects| / |all existing objects| alongside the CHAIR metrics.

The POPE evaluation is implemented as a binary yes/no VQA task across 3000 questions (500 images each from MSCOCO, A-OKVQA, and GQA datasets) in adversarial, popular, and random settings, reporting accuracy, F1 score, and yes ratio.
Prompt templates specific to the POPE task (Table 9) are implemented with restrictions instructing the model to answer based only on detected objects in the list.

The CHAIR evaluation is run on 500 randomly sampled MSCOCO images using the prompt 'Generate a short caption of the image.' with batch sizes per model (LLaVA=16, LLaVA-v1.5=4, MiniGPTv2=32, mPLUG-Owl2=16, InstructBLIP=16) and left padding for batched generation.

The CHAIR evaluation has been executed successfully for all five LVLMs (LLaVA, LLaVA-v1.5, MiniGPT-v2, mPLUG-Owl2, InstructBLIP) with all baselines (Greedy, LURE, Woodpecker, VCD, OPERA) and MARINE without errors.
The POPE evaluation has been executed successfully for all five LVLMs in the adversarial setting across MSCOCO, A-OKVQA, and GQA datasets without errors.

The GPT-4V-aided evaluation has been run using the LLaVA-QA90 task (90 questions) and image captioning task (50 MSCOCO images), scoring accuracy and detailedness on a 10-point scale for LLaVA and mPLUG-Owl2.
The CHAIR results match Table 1: MARINE achieves the best or near-best average CHAIRS and CHAIRI scores among all baselines, with average CHAIRS=8.4 and CHAIRI=3.7 across five LVLMs.
The CHAIR results for MARINE on LLaVA match Table 1: CHAIRS=17.8, CHAIRI=7.2, Recall=50.8, which is lower than Greedy (CHAIRS=26.6) and all other baselines.
The POPE adversarial results match Table 2: MARINE achieves average accuracy=79.9% and F1=80.4% across five LVLMs, with a yes ratio closer to 50% (51.1%) than the baselines.
The POPE results for MARINE on LLaVA-v1.5 match Table 2: accuracy=85.0%, F1=84.3%, yes ratio=45.7%, which outperforms Woodpecker (80.5%, 80.6%, 50.5%).
The GPT-4V-aided evaluation results match Table 3: MARINE-enhanced models receive consistently higher accuracy scores (e.g., LLaVA image captioning 5.27→6.11, mPLUG-Owl2 image captioning 7.97→8.63).
The agent has read the ablation study in Section 5.3 to understand the comparison between using DETR only, RAM++ only, and both models combined (intersection) for guidance.
The ablation experiment comparing DETR-only, RAM++-only, and their intersection is planned and implemented, evaluating CHAIRS and CHAIRI on LLaVA, LLaVA-v1.5, and mPLUG-Owl2.

The ablation for single vs. combined vision models has been executed and results match Table 6: MARINE (combined) outperforms MARINE-DETR only (CHAIRS=27.6 on LLaVA) and MARINE-RAM only (CHAIRS=29.0 on LLaVA) with CHAIRS=17.8.
The agent has read the integration method ablation in Section 5.3 (Table 7) to understand that intersection-based aggregation consistently outperforms union-based aggregation.
The ablation comparing intersection vs. union aggregation of DETR and RAM++ outputs has been implemented and executed on LLaVA, LLaVA-v1.5, and mPLUG-Owl2.
The union-based aggregation results match Table 7: MARINE-union has higher hallucination than MARINE-intersection (e.g., LLaVA: union CHAIRS=30.4 vs. intersection CHAIRS=17.8).
The guidance strength ablation has been implemented by varying γ from 0 to 1 and evaluating CHAIR metrics (CHAIRS, CHAIRI) on LLaVA, LLaVA-v1.5, and mPLUG-Owl2.
The guidance strength ablation results match Figure 3: increasing γ from 0 to 1 leads to a consistent decrease in CHAIR scores, indicating higher guidance strength reduces hallucination.
The latency comparison has been implemented by measuring inference latency (ms/token) for MARINE and all baselines on LLaVA-7B using 500 MSCOCO images with batch size=1 and max token length=64.

The latency results match Table 5: MARINE achieves 52.2 ms/token (×1.98 vs. greedy's 26.3 ms/token), the lowest overhead among all non-greedy baselines (OPERA=185.1 ms, LURE=179.9 ms, Woodpecker=94.5 ms, VCD=53.4 ms).

The POPE evaluation across three datasets (MSCOCO, A-OKVQA, GQA) in all three settings (random, popular, adversarial) is run and results match Table 4: MARINE consistently improves accuracy and F1 while reducing yes ratio across datasets.

The text quality evaluation (BLEU, ROUGE-L, CIDEr, SPICE) is implemented for the image captioning task on 500 MSCOCO images and LLaVA-QA90 task (90 questions), using GPT-4 text-only responses as references for LLaVA-QA90 and MSCOCO captions for image captioning.

The text quality results for MARINE match Tables 17-18: MARINE improves or maintains BLEU/ROUGE/CIDEr/SPICE scores for LLaVA on MSCOCO captioning (e.g., BLEU_1: 14.06→18.59) while mPLUG-Owl2 scores remain comparable.

The MARINE-Truth variant (using ground-truth MSCOCO object annotations as oracle guidance instead of DETR/RAM++) is implemented and evaluated on CHAIR and POPE metrics to show the upper bound of MARINE's performance.

The MARINE-Truth results match Tables 1-2: MARINE-Truth achieves the lowest CHAIRI scores (e.g., 5.1 on LLaVA, 2.5 on LLaVA-v1.5) and highest POPE accuracy (e.g., 92.0% on LLaVA-v1.5, 93.4% on mPLUG-Owl2), demonstrating the potential upper bound.
